LHX.sentence: L3Harris is up a bit today, but it's now trading at a much higher premium to other defense companies than usual, which historically isn't where you want to buy it.

ASML.sentence: ASML is holding steady — still the only company making those advanced chipmaking machines the world needs, though the stock isn't cheap by any measure.

LIN.sentence: Linde is quietly grinding higher as usual, trading at a small premium to its industrial gas competitors but nothing dramatic.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft is up slightly ahead of earnings, where investors will be looking for signs that Azure's AI business is really accelerating.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher is up nearly 3% as early signs suggest their bioprocessing business — which supplies the tools biotech companies need — might be hitting bottom after a tough stretch.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is having a quiet day, though the pipeline company's dividend yield has gotten so low compared to Treasury bonds that it's not offering much extra income anymore.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow jumped over 4% as investors seem convinced the company's AI automation tools will keep growing even if the stock price comes down from expensive levels.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore is down about 2% and lagging behind other copper-focused investments, despite the fact that copper makes up a big chunk of what they mine and trade.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin is down nearly 2% and sitting well below its recent highs, which some see as a potential buying opportunity after the big run-up.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA is flat today, with the Mexican peso holding steady against the dollar — which matters a lot for this Latin American bottler's profits.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is down slightly, but with oil prices near $96, the Brazilian oil giant is generating plenty of cash to support its hefty dividend payments.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk is up a bit today, with the shipping giant's fleet still trading below what it would cost to replace those container ships.
